name: Masood ibn Hajjaj
title: Devoted Father
shortDescription: A brave Kufan who, along with his son, came with Umar Sa'd's army but joined the Imam and both were martyred together.
fullStory:
Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj al-Taymi was a brave and well-known horseman of Kufa. Initially, he and his young son (Abdul Rahman) came to Karbala with Ibn Sa'd's army.

However, their goal was not to fight Imam Hussain (AS). They were waiting for an opportunity to escape the strict siege and control of Umar Sa'd's soldiers to join the Master of Martyrs' (AS) camp.

In the days before Ashura, they managed to separate from the enemy army and settle in the Imam's camp. As a devoted father, Mas'ud practically taught his son the lesson of loyalty.

On Ashura, during the extensive morning shower of arrows (the first attack), Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj and his son fought valiantly together and attained the great success of martyrdom side by side.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلَى مَسْعُودِ بْنِ حَجّاجٍ التَّيْمِيِّ...
quote: (Ziyarat al-Nahiya:) Peace be upon Mas'ud ibn Hajjaj al-Taymi...
